summary: At recommended 1% weight, ACHR contributes -44.3 bp to portfolio return, increases volatility by 261 bp, and accounts for only 1.55% of total portfolio risk—a highly inefficient risk-return tradeoff that destroys 27% of portfolio Sharpe ratio. Recommendation: AVOID unless holding for explicit thematic conviction on simultaneous execution of Abu Dhabi launch, FAA Type Cert, and battery confirmation (15% combined probability).
recommendation: AVOID or minimal 0.5-1.0% tactical satellite only

extras.interpretation: 1% position adds -44.3 bp return drag to portfolio; equivalent to purchasing a bond yielding 3.06% (44.3 bp below 3.5% risk-free rate). Position reduces expected portfolio return from 7.0% to 6.487%, a 51.3 bp net loss to baseline 100% S&P allocation.
extras.source_reference: A6_1.md Step 2 Scenario B portfolio return calculation; A5_1.md probability-weighted expected return (-44.3%) from 15% bull (+33.1%) + 60% base (-49.7%) + 25% bear (-81.8%)

extras.interpretation: Portfolio volatility increases 261 bp (from 15.0% to 17.61%), a 17.4% relative increase in risk. This amplification reflects ACHR's 60% standalone volatility (4x baseline) combined with 0.50 correlation with S&P 500. Position creates outsized volatility footprint relative to its weight: 1% weight drives 2.61% volatility increase due to high standalone risk and cross-asset volatility amplification.
extras.marginal_contribution_interpretation: Each additional 1% weight in ACHR adds ~27.35 bp to portfolio volatility; at current 1% position, this represents portion of total 261 bp increase.
extras.source_calculation: σ_p = sqrt(w_ACHR² × σ_ACHR² + w_Baseline² × σ_Baseline² + 2 × w_ACHR × w_Baseline × ρ × σ_ACHR × σ_Baseline) = sqrt(0.000036 + 0.022052 + 0.00891) = sqrt(0.030998) = 17.61%
extras.source_reference: A6_1.md Step 2 Scenario B portfolio volatility calculation

rows.label: Contribution to Total Portfolio Risk (1% weight)

extras.interpretation: ACHR contributes 27.36 bp of the total 1761 bp portfolio risk, representing 1.55% of overall portfolio volatility. Despite 1% weight, position accounts for only 1.55% of risk due to moderate correlation (0.50) with baseline portfolio. Position exhibits positive diversification contribution relative to weight, but negative expected return eliminates any benefit from reduced risk exposure.
extras.covariance_calculation: Cov(ACHR, portfolio) = w_ACHR × σ_ACHR² + w_Baseline × ρ × σ_ACHR × σ_Baseline = 0.01 × (0.60)² + 0.99 × 0.50 × 0.60 × 0.15 = 0.0036 + 0.04455 = 0.04815
extras.beta_interpretation: ACHR has beta of 1.552 relative to the 17.61% volatility portfolio, meaning it amplifies portfolio volatility moves by 55.2% above portfolio average.
extras.source_reference: A6_1.md Marginal Contribution to Risk section; covariance-based decomposition

avoid_reasons: Negative expected return (-44.3%) disqualifies from Sharpe-maximizing allocation
Return drag (-44.3 bp) not compensated by diversification benefit (only 1.55% risk contribution)
High sensitivity to labor market shocks: +1σ wage inflation triggers 65% stock loss per A5.2
Bull case requires simultaneous execution on three forward drivers (15% probability per A5.1)
Market pricing near bull case outcomes at current $6.48; asymmetric downside risk
hold_justification: Explicit thematic/ESG aerospace exposure mandate required
Conviction on all three forward drivers: Abu Dhabi Q3 2026 launch, FAA Type Cert late 2026, battery 400 Wh/kg confirmation
Ability to tolerate 45%+ drawdown and accept quarterly rebalancing discipline
Portfolio mandate includes long-duration growth/optionality (bull case +33% upside)

increase_above_1pct_if: Abu Dhabi commercial launch confirmed operational with revenue (Q3 2026 milestone)
FAA Type Certification delivered (late 2026 vs. Q1 2027 base case)
Production ramp validated at 150+ aircraft annually (approaching base case execution)
Capital raise confirmed non-dilutive or at $7.00+ per share (Stellantis partnership locked)
decrease_to_zero_if: Abu Dhabi launch slips beyond Q4 2026 (probability-weighted transition to base/bear case)
FAA Type Cert slips >6 months (Q2 2027 or later; competitive disadvantage vs. Joby per A4.2)
Production ramp misses 100 aircraft run rate (cascades to capital crisis per A5.2)
Additional $500M+ dilutive capital raise at <$6.00/share (shareholder dilution trigger)

joby_aviation.recommendation: PREFERRED if constrained to eVTOL allocation; same 0-1% cap applies
joby_aviation.rationale: 60% probability of outperforming Archer per A4.3; FAA Type Cert regulatory advantage (6-12 months ahead) de-risks timeline vs. Archer's UAE GCAA pathway
joby_aviation.caveat: Joby also has negative expected return; not recommended for core allocation

ehang_holdings.rationale: Only profitable peer (EH revenue $72.9M, 10.3x EV/Revenue); autonomous cargo operations model de-risks passenger air taxi TAM concentration
ehang_holdings.caveat: Lower upside optionality; 5-10% expected returns vs. ACHR's +33% bull case

peer_comparison.default_action: If seeking eVTOL sector exposure, prioritize EHang (profitable, lower vol); avoid ACHR and Joby due to negative expected returns

data_sources.A6_1_md: Portfolio weight calculation and optimal allocation framework (Markowitz 2-asset optimization)
data_sources.A5_1_md: Three-scenario valuation model: probability-weighted expected return -44.3% (bull 15% +33.1%, base 60% -49.7%, bear 25% -81.8%)
data_sources.A5_2_md: Macro stress tests; base case sensitivity to wage inflation (65% loss at +1σ shock); Sharpe calculations
data_sources.A3_3_md: Regulatory timeline: Abu Dhabi Q3 2026 GCAA RTC pathway, FAA Type Cert late 2026, production ramp 252 aircraft 2027 target
data_sources.A4_1_md: Peer comparison: Joby (55-79x EV/Revenue, FAA Type Cert advantage), EHang (profitable 10.3x multiple), Beta (OEM backlog not comparable)
data_sources.A4_3_md: Probability ranking: 60% Joby > Archer, 55% Archer > Joby; regulatory timeline de-risking drives Joby preference
data_sources.A2_3_md: Forward drivers and capital requirements; Stellantis $400M partnership non-dilutive path assumption
